note: Definitions are independently authored football meanings. The generator must consume only definition text, not concept ids, gold chains, catalog refs, or known provider paths.

definitions.text: A give-and-go is a two-pass combination where the first passer plays to a teammate and then receives the return pass back as the terminal receiver.
A wall-pass sequence has the ball leave the original passer, get relayed by a teammate, and come back to that same original passer.

perturbations.text: A two-pass sequence where the first passer plays to a teammate and the teammate continues the ball onward to a different terminal receiver.

definitions.text: Receive under pressure means the ball receiver is under observed defender pressure at the reception moment.
A reception under pressure occurs when the player receiving the ball has nearest-defender pressure at the receiving frame.
Carry out of pressure means the same carrier starts a carry under pressure and ends the carry with pressure reduced.
A player carries the ball from pressure into less pressure when the pressure distance improves between carry start and carry end.
Shape expansion means team shape width or depth increases across the same anchor after the observed moment.
A team-shape expansion occurs when the measured team width or depth gets larger after the anchor than before it.
Expected pass completion is the model probability that a pass will be completed.
Pass completion expectation means a learned likelihood or probability of a completed pass.
Goalkeeper set position proxy depends on the goalkeeper's body orientation and stance before the action.
A keeper set-position proxy asks whether the goalkeeper is facing and set in a particular stance.
Goal-kick build structure means the team shape and first build pattern from a goal kick restart.
Goal kick build-up structure describes the restart shape and build pattern after the goal kick is taken.
